name: Butter Chicken
tagline: The world's most-ordered Indian dish, made properly

hero.alt: Butter chicken in a cream ceramic bowl with charred chicken pieces, cream swirl, and kasuri methi
hero.caption: butter chicken — charred chicken, makhani sauce

steps.title: Marinate the chicken
steps.detail: Score the chicken pieces deeply with a knife. Mix yogurt, Kashmiri chili, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, garam masala, and salt. Coat the chicken thoroughly and marinate for at least 30 minutes — overnight in the fridge is the real move.
steps.tip: Cold marinade = tighter texture at first. Pull chicken out 20 min before cooking for even heat.

steps.title: Sear the chicken
steps.detail: Grill on a hot grill pan (or broil at 220°C) for 12–15 minutes, turning once. You want char marks and slightly smoky edges. This 'tandoor effect' is what separates butter chicken from just tomato-cream chicken.
steps.tip: Don't crowd the pan. Space = steam escape = char, not steam.

steps.title: Build the makhani base
steps.detail: In 2 tbsp butter, sauté onion until deep golden (8–9 min). Add soaked cashews, tomatoes, and 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste. Cook down for 10 minutes until tomatoes are jammy and the oil starts to separate.
steps.tip: The key is patience — underdone tomatoes make the gravy taste raw and sharp.

steps.title: Blend and strain
steps.detail: Cool the base slightly. Blend until completely smooth. Strain through a fine mesh for restaurant-silk texture. This step is optional but transforms the dish.
steps.tip: A high-speed blender makes straining optional — just blend very smooth.

steps.title: Simmer the gravy
steps.detail: In 2 tbsp butter, cook the strained gravy on low heat for 8 minutes. Add sugar and salt. The gravy should reduce and deepen to a rich tomato-red that coats a spoon.
steps.tip: Low and slow is the key. High heat makes it splatter and burns the butter.

steps.title: Add chicken and finish
steps.detail: Add the grilled chicken pieces to the gravy. Pour in cream. Crush kasuri methi between your palms and add — this activates its essential oils. Simmer gently for 5 more minutes.
steps.tip: If gravy is too thick, add a splash of warm water — never cold, it'll break the sauce.

healthFacts: Chicken provides ~31g protein per 100g — one of the most complete animal protein sources.
Yogurt marinade tenderizes the meat and contributes gut-friendly probiotics.
Kashmiri chili gives the signature red color with minimal capsaicin heat — lycopene-rich.
Tomatoes cooked in fat release more lycopene — one of the few cases where cooking helps.
Swap heavy cream for Greek yogurt stirred in off-heat to cut saturated fat by ~50%.
Kasuri methi helps regulate blood sugar and adds a fenugreek depth the sauce needs.
